text:
Once upon a time in a small forest village, there lived a curious old owl named Hedwig. Hedwig was known far and wide for her endless curiosity, often diving into the most inconsequential affairs.

One day, while perching on a low branch of an ancient oak, Hedwig heard a rustle in the leaves. Peering closer, she saw a squirrel playing hide-and-seek. Hedwig couldn't resist participating. So, she scampered down into the valley below, her piercing eye-tracking catching the adorable squirrel.

Now, squirrels in the village weren't accustomed to nocturnal tourists. Hedwig's fluffy tail swung unpredictably as she chased the little buddy around. The villagers laughed, watching the owl’s antics. After several failed attempts, the squirrel cleverly climbed a branch.

"Come on!" Hedwig hooted. "That's no fair! Didn't you see I've got wings?"
